speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thank you very much, Jonathan. It was a record third quarter powered by the continued strength of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Microsoft Cloud which exceeded $54 billion in revenue up 29% year over year. Our AI business surpassed
speaker: OPERATOR

text: $37 billion ARR up 123%. We are at the beginning of one of the most consequential platform shifts that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: will change the entire tech stack as agents proliferate and become the dominant workload.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: This will drive time expansion and change the value creation equation across the entire economy.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: To capture this opportunity, we are executing against two priorities. First, we are building
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the world's leading cloud and AI infrastructure for a genetic computing error. Second, we are
speaker: OPERATOR

text: building high value agent systems across core domains such as productivity, coding and security.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: These two layers reinforce each other and we are focused on driving competitive value and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: differentiation for customers across each so that they can eval max their outcomes. Today, I'll
speaker: OPERATOR

text: focus my remarks on both priorities starting with infrastructure. We're optimizing every layer of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the tech stack from DC design to silicon to system software, the model architecture as well as its
speaker: OPERATOR

text: optimization. This is translating into operational gains. We have reduced doctor lifetimes for new
speaker: OPERATOR

text: GPUs in our biggest regions by nearly 20% since the beginning of the year. Our Fairwater Data
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Center in Wisconsin came online earlier this month, six weeks ahead of schedule allowing us to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: recognize revenue earlier and we delivered a 40% improvement in inference throughput for our most
speaker: OPERATOR

text: used models across co-pilot driven by our software and hardware optimization work.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: All up, we added in another gigawatt of capacity this quarter and remain on track to double our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: overall footprint in just two years. We're moving aggressively to add capacity aligned to our demand
speaker: OPERATOR

text: signals we see and we have announced new data center investments across four continents.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We also continue to modernize our fleet with our first party innovation alongside the latest from
speaker: OPERATOR

text: NVIDIA and AMD. Across our fleet, millions of servers are powered by our custom networking
speaker: OPERATOR

text: security and virtualization silicon including Azure Boost as well as our first party CPUs and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: accelerators. Our Maya 200 AI accelerator which offers over 30% improved tokens per dollar compared
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to the latest silicon in our fleet is now live in our Iowa and Arizona data centers.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Our cobalt server CPU is deployed in nearly half of our DC regions running workloads at scale for
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers like Databricks, Siemens and Snowflake. As our largest customers scale their AI deployments,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: they're increasingly leveraging other services across our platform and choosing to run those
speaker: OPERATOR

text: workloads on cobalt. And we're expanding cobalt supply significantly to meet this demand.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The next layer up from infrastructure is the agent app platform. It starts with model choice.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We offer the broadest selection of models of any hyperscalers or customers can choose the right
speaker: OPERATOR

text: model for the right workload across OpenAI and Tropic, open source and more. Over 10,000 customers
speaker: OPERATOR

text: have used more than one model on Foundry, 5,000 have used open source models and the number
speaker: OPERATOR

text: who have used anthropic in OpenAI models increased to X quarter over quarter. For example,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: bear is using multiple models in Foundry to create its own in-house agent platform with more than
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 20,000 active monthly users. All up over 300 customers are on track to process over 1 trillion tokens
speaker: OPERATOR

text: on Foundry this year, accelerating 30% quarter over quarter. We also remain focused on our
speaker: OPERATOR

text: first party model work to differentiate our high value copilot and agents and reduce COGS.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: We introduced MAI transcribed one, a state-of-the-art speech-to-text model and MAI image two, one of the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: top image generation models in the world. These models are already powering first party scenarios
speaker: OPERATOR

text: like image generation in Bing and PowerPoint and we're working towards having transcribed one
speaker: OPERATOR

text: power transcription in copilot and teams. Early signals show 67% increase in GPU efficiency with
speaker: OPERATOR

text: transcribed one and up to 260% increase in image two. We also brought MAI models to commercial
speaker: OPERATOR

text: customers like Shutter Stock and WPP for the first time through Foundry. And we are innovating on
speaker: OPERATOR

text: OpenAI IP to drive product evals and lower COGS. Two recent examples of what we've done with
speaker: OPERATOR

text: multi-step retrieval with work IQ in copilot and how reasoning adapts to intent complexity and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: researcher with much reduced latency and increased accuracy. The next layer up is all about enterprise
speaker: OPERATOR

text: data in context across Fabric, Foundry, Microsoft 365 and our security graph. We are building a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: unified IQ layer for organizational intelligence. Thousands of enterprises already are accessing
speaker: OPERATOR

text: context across these IQ layers. And as AI usage grows, so does the context layer creating a fly
speaker: OPERATOR

text: wheel that continuously improves the grounding, relevance and effectiveness of every agent they
speaker: OPERATOR

text: use and build making our IQ layers an unmatched context engine for organizational intelligence.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: More broadly, our database business accelerated quarter over quarter. Cosmos DB alone saw 50%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year over year revenue growth driven by AI app workloads. We now have 35,000 paid fabric customers
speaker: OPERATOR

text: up 60% year over year. And all up the amount of data in Fabric 1. Lake data lake increased nearly
speaker: OPERATOR

text: 4x year over year. Over 15,000 customers now use both Foundry and Fabric up 60% year over year
speaker: OPERATOR

text: as enterprises connect agents to real-time operational, analytical and unstructured data
speaker: OPERATOR

text: that Fabric brings together. And we're very excited about the continued progress with Foundry
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agent service and how customers can now build durable stateful agents that run across time
speaker: OPERATOR

text: boundaries, orchestrate tools and models and close the loop with EVALS and improvement
speaker: OPERATOR

text: over long-running workflows. Beyond Fabric and Foundry, we're also helping knowledge workers
speaker: OPERATOR

text: build agents with tools like Copilot Studio. Nearly 90% of the Fortune 500 now have active
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agents built with our low-code, no-code tools. And we're seeing fast growth of our copilot credit
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consumptive offer up nearly 2x quarter over quarter as customers increasingly extend
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Copilot with custom agents tailored to their workflows. Finally, with Agent 365, we offer a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: control plane that extends companies existing governments, identity, security and management
speaker: OPERATOR

text: frameworks to agents. Tens of thousands of companies are already managing tens of millions of agents
speaker: OPERATOR

text: in Agent 365. And we expect this momentum to grow significantly as agents will increasingly need
speaker: OPERATOR

text: tools for identity governance, security and more. Now, let me turn to the high-value
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agent systems we ourselves are building on this platform. We are evolving our family of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Copilot from synchronous assistance to async co-workers that can execute long-running tasks
speaker: OPERATOR

text: across key domains. In knowledge work, it was another record quarter for Microsoft 365 Copilot
speaker: OPERATOR

text: seat ads, which increased 250% year over year representing our fastest growth since launch.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Quarter over quarter, we continue to see acceleration and now have over 20 million
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Microsoft 365 Copilot paid seats. The number of customers with over 50,000 seats quadrupled
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year over year and extension now has over 740,000 seats, our largest copilot win to date.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: And Bear, Johnson & Johnson, Mercedes and Roche all committed to 90,000 or more seats.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Copilot is uniquely valuable at work where nearly every task depends on organizational context.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Work IQ grounds, Copilot responses in the full context of an organization including people,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: roles, documents and communications all within the company's security boundary.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The system of work behind work IQ alone now spans more than 17 exabytes of data growing 35%
speaker: OPERATOR

text: year over year. The liquidity and freshness of that data matters with billions of emails,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: documents, chats, hundreds of millions of teams meetings and millions of SharePoint sites
speaker: OPERATOR

text: added each day. And that context is getting even richer as copilot adoption grows,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: copilot and agent conversations and artifacts that create feedback into work IQ making it even
speaker: OPERATOR

text: more context rich. We continue to increase the pace of feature innovation across Microsoft 365
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Copilot introducing over 625 updates over the past year up 50%. In Microsoft 365 Copilot,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: you now have access in chat to multiple models by default with intelligent auto routing in
speaker: OPERATOR

text: agents with critique and counsel. You can use multiple models together to generate optimal
speaker: OPERATOR

text: responses. As of last week, Asian mode is now default experience across copilot in Word, Excel
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and PowerPoint. And with co-work, you now have a new way to delegate and complete work using
speaker: OPERATOR

text: copilot. All this innovation is driving record usage intensity across copilot. We have seen a
speaker: OPERATOR

text: surge in usage of our first party agents with monthly active usage up 6x year to date.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Copilot queries per user were up nearly 20% quarter over quarter. To put this momentum in perspective,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: weekly engagement is now at the same level as outlook as more and more users make copilot a habit.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: When it comes to Biz apps, we are seeing a new pattern emerge as customer shift from traditional
speaker: OPERATOR

text: seat model to seats plus consumption. The customer service category is at the forefront
speaker: OPERATOR

text: of this transformation as nearly 60% of our service customers are already purchasing usage-based
speaker: OPERATOR

text: credits. For example, it just BC uses prebuilt agents with Dynamics 365 to manage customer
speaker: OPERATOR

text: inquiries across products, markets, regulatory requirements, reducing issue resolution time
speaker: OPERATOR

text: by over 30%. And our agent products in LinkedIn talent solutions, which help hire us
speaker: OPERATOR

text: automate time-consuming tasks like sourcing, screening and drafting messages, have already
speaker: OPERATOR

text: surpassed a $450 million annualized revenue run rate. When it comes to developers, get up
speaker: OPERATOR

text: itself is seeing unprecedented growth driven by proliferation of agent coding. And we are hard
speaker: OPERATOR

text: at work to scale and meet this demand. We see this even with get up copilot. Nearly 140,000
speaker: OPERATOR

text: organizations now use get up copilot and enterprise subscribers have nearly tripled year over year.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: The majority of users leverage multiple models. We are also seeing rapid adoption of get up copilot
speaker: OPERATOR

text: CLI with usage nearly doubling month over month. In earlier this week, we announced our move to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: usage-based pricing model for get up copilot as we align pricing to actual usage and costs.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: When it comes to security, the physics and cybersecurity has changed as AI compresses the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: window between vulnerability and exploitation. To help mitigate risk immediately, we simship
speaker: OPERATOR

text: defender protections when updates for AI discovered vulnerabilities are released. And we are on
speaker: OPERATOR

text: course to productize new multi-moral AI driven scanning harness as well. Already the number of
speaker: OPERATOR

text: security copilot customers increase to X, year over year. Our data security triage agents alone
speaker: OPERATOR

text: handled over 2 million unique alerts this quarter. And we are helping customers secure their AI
speaker: OPERATOR

text: deployments as well. 35 billion copilot interactions have been audited by per view to date up 7X year
speaker: OPERATOR

text: over year. Finally, when it comes to our consumer business, we're doing the foundational work
speaker: OPERATOR

text: required to win back fans and strengthen engagement across Windows, Xbox, Bing and Edge. In the near
speaker: OPERATOR

text: term, we are focused on fundamentals prioritizing quality and serving our core users better. You see
speaker: OPERATOR

text: this in the work underway across our consumer products with Windows. We recently announced
speaker: OPERATOR

text: performance improvements for lower memory devices, streamlined the Windows update experience and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: brought back focus to core features and fundamentals that matter most to our customers. And you also
speaker: OPERATOR

text: see this in Xbox where the team is recommitting to our core fans and players and shaping the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: future of play. Last week's game pass changes are one example of how we are staying responsive
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to customer feedback. Monthly active Windows devices surpassed 1.6 billion and over time
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Windows value will extend to deliver unmetered intelligence at the edge. Our Edge browser has
speaker: OPERATOR

text: taken share for 20 consecutive quarters and Bing monthly active users reached 1 billion for the first
speaker: OPERATOR

text: time. LinkedIn has 1.3 billion members and we are seeing increased depth of conversation and it's
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the leading B2B sales and advertising channel for large and small businesses. We set new records
speaker: OPERATOR

text: for monthly Xbox active users in the quarter as well as game streaming hours and in Microsoft 365
speaker: OPERATOR

text: consumer we now have nearly 95 million subscribers and early signals show increasing satisfaction
speaker: OPERATOR

text: as we make agent mode the default. Across everything I've talked about, we're also hard at work
speaker: OPERATOR

text: changing the way we work. Our North Star remains the same delivering customer value with highest
speaker: OPERATOR

text: quality and top-class innovation and this is what gives me confidence in our ability to shape the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: next face of growth for our company and our customers. With that let me turn it over to Amy to walk
speaker: OPERATOR

text: through our financial results and outlook. Thank you Satya and good afternoon everyone.

text: Thanks, Amy. One of the big pushbacks we all get is that AI is going to be really expensive.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Yet you, Google and Amazon are showing higher margins tonight as you report. What are investors
speaker: OPERATOR

text: missing and why is AI a potential better margin for the industry over time?
speaker: OPERATOR

text: Thanks, Brent. I think we've been talking about sort of where this AI business of ours has been
speaker: OPERATOR

text: in the cycle compared to even the cycle we saw with the cloud, which now seems very long ago,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: and how margins were actually better and they've remained better in our AI business versus where
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we saw in the cloud transition looking back. And so I do feel like what we've been really focused
speaker: OPERATOR

text: on is making sure that the business models reflect how these applications are both getting built and
speaker: OPERATOR

text: the value that they're bringing. And so when you think about that type of value, it tends to be
speaker: OPERATOR

text: captured more in consumption and usage-based pricing models. And I think that's something that's
speaker: OPERATOR

text: probably been a little underappreciated as we look in terms of margins going forward.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: I also think it's been important to us to make sure we leverage the IP we have,

text: the IP we get from our partnerships is obviously free to us for a long time. So we're able to
speaker: OPERATOR

text: take that and apply it and to benefit our margins in a healthy way. You've also seen this work hard
speaker: OPERATOR

text: on the first-party hardware stack being able to make sure we can take margins out of the
speaker: OPERATOR

text: infrastructure stack as well. And then of course just the efficiency work. We've really been in an
speaker: OPERATOR

text: accelerated phase, as you know, of trying to get as much capacity as we can get into production.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: But when you go through that, you also then start to focus on the efficiency work.
speaker: OPERATOR

text: That's efficiency work on the hardware side as well as efficiency work on the software side
speaker: OPERATOR

text: to be able to deliver these types of margins. I do believe that one of the real focuses that
speaker: OPERATOR

text: we've got to all have is that, and it really dates back to the question, the key task in the beginning,
speaker: OPERATOR

text: which is that when you move to usage-based models, you have to make sure you're delivering

text: incredibly high value to customers. So what we need to do is make sure the focus starts with

speaker: OPERATOR
